I love it.","location":"Dealer"},{"id":"e124","key":"event-c5-2026-6-14","calendarId":"c5","year":2026,"month":5,"day":14,"name":"Wedding anniversary","time":"Evening","notes":"15 years. She still laughs at my jokes. Low bar but I'll take it. Surprise TBD.","location":"TBD"}]
journal-j1-2026-1-10:
**Lesson:** Never schedule a 7am call the day after your kid's sleepover. The investors can wait. My brain cannot.

Also: coffee is not a substitute for sleep. It's a *supplement*.
journal-j1-2026-1-15:
Pitch went well! Key takeaway: **simplicity wins**.

Cut the deck from 45 to 12 slides. One VC said it was the clearest pitch he'd seen.

Note to self: less is more.
journal-j1-2026-1-22:
Dad's birthday dinner. He told the same story about the 1987 market crash for the 47th time.

I've learned to just nod and say "that's wild, Dad."

He's happy. I'm present. That's the lesson.
journal-j1-2026-2-5:
**Big lesson today:** Saying "no" to a meeting request is not unprofessional.

Saying "yes" and then resenting it for 3 days is.

Boundaries = sanity.
journal-j1-2026-2-14:
Valentine's with Sarah. Lesson: *always* make the reservation a week early.

We ended up at the place with the sticky menus.

Still romantic. But sticky.
journal-j1-2026-3-10:
Product launch day! Lesson: celebrate the wins.

We shipped. It's not perfect. Nothing ever is. But we shipped.

Team dinner tonight.
journal-j1-2026-3-15:
Jake's 10th birthday party. 15 ten-year-olds. Chaos.

Lesson: **hire a magician next time.** Or a bouncy castle. Or both.

Definitely both.
journal-j1-2026-4-15:
Tax day survived.

Lesson: hire a good accountant. Worth every penny.

Also: extensions are your friend. No shame in the extension game.
journal-j1-2026-5-1:
May Day. Took the afternoon off.

Lesson: **rest is productivity.** Came back with 3 ideas that had been eluding me for weeks.

The brain needs idle time.
journal-j1-2026-6-18:
Emma's unicorn party.

Lesson: 7-year-olds have *opinions* about unicorn cake. Very strong opinions.

The rainbow frosting was non-negotiable.
journal-j1-2026-7-15:
Board meeting.

Lesson: quarterly reviews are exhausting but necessary. The numbers don't lie.

Neither does my need for a nap after.
journal-j1-2026-8-5: Hawaii day 5. Lesson: **disconnecting is a skill.** Took 4 days to stop checking Slack. On day 5, I finally felt human. Need to do this more often.
journal-j1-2026-9-16:
Team offsite day 2.

Lesson: the best ideas come from whiteboard sessions, not slide decks.

Also: free lunch doesn't mean you have to eat 3 sandwiches. I learned that the hard way.
journal-j1-2026-10-31:
Halloween with the kids.

Lesson: walking the neighborhood in a costume is a great way to meet neighbors.

Also: I make a decent zombie. Sarah said "too realistic." I'll take it.
journal-j1-2026-11-26:
Thanksgiving at mom's.

Lesson: **family dynamics don't change.** Uncle Bob will still argue about politics. I will still hide in the kitchen.

Some things are tradition.
journal-j1-2026-12-25:
Christmas.

Lesson: the best gifts aren't wrapped. Jake's face when we got him the bike. Emma's when she saw the puppy (we got a dog!).

Worth every chaotic moment.
journal-j2-2026-1-1:
1. Fresh start. New year energy.

2. Coffee. Always coffee.

3. That the kids slept past 7am. Small miracles.
journal-j2-2026-1-8:
1. Sarah's birthday — 15 years together.

2. She said yes to me. Still can't believe it.

3. The cake. She picked a good one.
journal-j2-2026-1-20:
1. The pitch went through. We're funded!

2. The team that believed when it was just an idea.

3. Sarah's "I told you so" face. She earned it.
journal-j2-2026-2-14:
1. Valentine's with my person.

2. Sticky menus and all — we laughed.

3. That we still make each other laugh after 15 years.
journal-j2-2026-2-24:
1. Last day of ski trip. No broken bones!

2. Jake finally made it down the blue run.

3. Hot chocolate by the fire. Perfect.
journal-j2-2026-3-10:
1. We shipped. We actually shipped.

2. The team. Every single one of them.

3. That I get to build something that matters.
journal-j2-2026-3-15:
1. Jake is 10. Double digits. How?

2. The magician we hired. Worth every penny.

3. That my parents could be there.
journal-j2-2026-4-5:
1. Mom's birthday brunch.

2. She's healthy. She's happy.

3. That I can take her to her favorite place.
journal-j2-2026-5-25:
1. Lake house with the family.

2. The kids are finally old enough to fish without losing the pole.

3. Quiet mornings with Sarah before the chaos.
journal-j2-2026-6-18:
1. Emma's 7. Unicorn everything.

2. Her laugh. Best sound in the world.

3. That we could give her the party she wanted.
journal-j2-2026-6-21:
1. Father's Day. Golf with dad.

2. He still beats me. Every time.

3. That he's here to beat me.
journal-j2-2026-7-4:
1. Fireworks with the family.

2. Freedom. In every sense.

3. That the kids still say "wow" at the same parts.
journal-j2-2026-8-7:
1. Hawaii. We made it.

2. Sarah in the sun. She's glowing.

3. That we could do this. Together.
journal-j2-2026-9-7:
1. Labor Day. Day off.

2. Grilled. Napped. Repeat.

3. No emails. Glorious.
journal-j2-2026-10-31:
1. Kids in costumes. Adorable.

2. Our street goes all out. Community.

3. Leftover candy. Obviously.
journal-j2-2026-11-26:
1. Thanksgiving. Full table.

2. Mom's cooking. Nothing compares.

3. Everyone under one roof. Loud. Perfect.
journal-j2-2026-12-25:
1. We got a dog. We're officially that family.

2. The kids' faces. Priceless.

3. Sarah. For putting up with "one more thing" for 15 years.
journal-j3-2026-1-5:
Need to remember: call dentist for annual checkup.

Also: Jake has science fair in March — start the volcano project early.

Last year's papier-mâché disaster will not be repeated.
journal-j3-2026-1-18:
Random thought: why do we say "running late" when we're not running?

We're usually walking fast and stressed. "Stressed walking" doesn't have the same ring though.
journal-j3-2026-2-8:
Sarah's idea: monthly date night rotation. We pick from a jar.

This month: mini golf. I will destroy her.

She doesn't know I was the Putt-Putt champion of 2003. (Our local mall. Don't judge.)
journal-j3-2026-2-28:
Emma asked why the sky is blue. I gave the scientific answer.

She said "oh" and asked for a snack.

Priorities. I respect it.
journal-j3-2026-3-22:
Dad called. Wanted to "pick my brain" about his 401k. I explained index funds.

He said "so it's like a slow race?"

Yes, Dad. Exactly like a slow race.
journal-j3-2026-4-12:
Jake's teacher says he's "a natural leader."

I think that's code for "talks a lot."

Either way, proud dad moment. Bought him ice cream.
journal-j3-2026-5-15:
Meeting notes: VC asked about our "moat." I said we have great coffee. He didn't laugh.

Note to self: rehearse the real answer.
journal-j3-2026-6-5:
Emma drew a family portrait. I have a very large head.

She said "because you have big ideas, Daddy."

I'm choosing to take that as a compliment.
journal-j3-2026-7-20:
Sarah and I had an actual conversation that wasn't about logistics. It's been a minute.

Need to do that more. She's funny. I forget that sometimes.
journal-j3-2026-8-15:
Back from Hawaii. The inbox is a war zone. 847 unread.

I'm not mad. I'm impressed. That's dedication from my team to wait for me.
journal-j3-2026-9-1:
September. Back to school. The house is quiet from 8-3.

I don't hate it. I might love it.

Don't tell the kids.
journal-j3-2026-10-15:
Mom asked when we're visiting for the holidays. I said we'd figure it out.

She said "I'll hold you to it."

She will. She always does.
journal-j3-2026-11-10:
Jake wants a dog for Christmas. Emma wants a unicorn. We're getting a dog.

Emma will adapt. Unicorns aren't real.

(Don't tell her I said that.)
journal-j3-2026-12-20:
Pre-Christmas chaos. Wrapping. Cooking. Hiding presents.

The dog keeps finding them. We might have a problem.

A very cute, tail-wagging problem.
